explanationZhTw: 小心翼翼地前進、選路而行；這裡不是單純的「挑選」，而是指因為路況或環境不方便，所以謹慎地一步一步走。
generatedExample: We picked our way across the rocks after the rain.

explanationZhTw: 斑駁的、點狀光影的；常用來形容陽光穿過樹葉後，在地面形成一塊一塊的光影。
generatedExample: The garden was covered in dappled sunlight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻說法，指一塊塊像金色池水般的光斑；不是字面上的「金色池子」。
generatedExample: At sunset, the lake looked like pools of gold.

explanationZhTw: 樹枝，尤其是較粗的樹枝或大枝幹；屬於較文學性的用法。
generatedExample: Birds rested on the boughs above the path.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指真的有霧，而是形容地面被藍鈴花覆蓋，看起來像一層朦朧的藍色霧氣。
generatedExample: The valley was misty with morning fog.

explanationZhTw: 森林深處；heart 在這裡是比喻，表示某個地方的中心或最裡面。
generatedExample: The cabin was hidden in the heart of the forest.

explanationZhTw: 低沉單調的聲音；這裡用來形容斑鳩持續發出的聲音。
generatedExample: I could hear the droning of insects outside the tent.

explanationZhTw: 「evidently experienced」表示「顯然很有經驗」。這裡不是單純描述，而是在強調她看起來熟悉情況、知道怎麼應對。
generatedExample: She was evidently experienced and stayed calm when the plan changed.

explanationZhTw: 「safe place」指「安全的地方」，也就是不容易被發現或危險較低的地點。
generatedExample: We need to find a safe place to wait out the storm.

explanationZhTw: 「assume」是「假定、認為」。這裡表示不要先入為主地以為鄉下就一定比較安全。
generatedExample: I assumed he had already left, but he was still in the office.

explanationZhTw: 「concealed microphones」是「隱藏的麥克風」。這裡指被偷偷裝設、很難發現的監聽設備。
generatedExample: They worried that concealed microphones had been placed in the room.

explanationZhTw: 「picked up」在這裡不是「撿起」，而是「被接收、被偵測到」。也就是聲音可能被麥克風收進去。
generatedExample: The hidden recorder picked up every word they said.

explanationZhTw: 「endorsed」在護照語境中是「加蓋核准、註記」。這裡指護照不必再經過正式蓋章或批准。
generatedExample: Her passport had to be endorsed before she could travel.

explanationZhTw: 「hanging about」是「閒晃、逗留」。這裡表示巡邏隊可能在車站附近徘徊等待。
generatedExample: A few teenagers were hanging about outside the store.

explanationZhTw: 「awkward questions」在這裡不是「尷尬的問題」而已，而是「難回答、麻煩的問題」。
generatedExample: The reporter asked some awkward questions about the delay.

explanationZhTw: 「made sure」是「確認、確保」。這裡表示他反覆查看，確定沒有人跟蹤。
generatedExample: I made sure the door was locked before I left.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」是小說中的專有用法，指工人階級或下層大眾，帶有特定社會階層意味。
generatedExample: In the novel, the proles live very different lives from the Party members.

explanationZhTw: 「holiday mood」表示「假日心情、放鬆愉快的氣氛」。這裡是指人們因天氣好而顯得輕鬆。
generatedExample: Everyone was in a holiday mood after the last exam ended.

explanationZhTw: 「get hold of」是「弄到、取得」。這裡表示他們想設法買到一點奶油。
generatedExample: I finally got hold of her after trying all morning.

explanationZhTw: 「black-market」是「黑市的、非法交易的」。這裡表示那塊奶油不是合法買來的，而是私下違規取得的。
generatedExample: They were caught selling black-market cigarettes.

explanationZhTw: 指「小徑、步道」；這裡是鄉間狹窄可走的小路。
generatedExample: We followed a footpath through the trees.

explanationZhTw: 指牛走出來的窄土路，語氣比 footpath 更粗糙、鄉野。
generatedExample: The car bounced along a cattle-track to the farm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「跳進去」，而是指道路或小徑突然往前、往下延伸進灌木叢中。
generatedExample: The road plunged down into the valley.

explanationZhTw: 指「腳下」的位置，常用來描述地面或腳踩到的地方。
generatedExample: Be careful, the rocks are slippery underfoot.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指打發時間、消磨時間。
generatedExample: She read magazines to pass the time away.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「湊起、 հավաք集」的意思，指把花束整理成一大束。
generatedExample: We got together a small team for the project.

explanationZhTw: 比喻用法，表示他突然僵住、嚇得動彈不得。
generatedExample: The sudden noise froze him for a second.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，英式用法，指回頭看、轉身看。
generatedExample: She looked round when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面「顯示罪惡」，而是「顯得心虛、有罪惡感」。
generatedExample: His nervous smile seemed to show guilt.

explanationZhTw: 表示手輕輕落在肩上，強調動作很輕柔。
generatedExample: A snowflake fell lightly on her coat.

explanationZhTw: 「keep silent」在這裡是「保持安靜、不要出聲」的意思，屬於比較口語的命令式說法。
generatedExample: Please keep silent during the presentation.

explanationZhTw: 「led the way」意思是「帶路、引路」，指她走在前面並帶著他前進。
generatedExample: The guide led the way through the forest.

explanationZhTw: 「boggy bits」指「泥濘、濕軟像沼澤的地方」；這裡是說她很熟悉地形，所以會避開那些難走的地面。
generatedExample: Be careful of the boggy bits near the river.

explanationZhTw: 「draw back」意思是「退縮、往後縮」，常用來表示因害怕、猶豫或不安而後退。
generatedExample: She drew back when she saw the dog.

explanationZhTw: 「daunted」是「使...感到畏懼、卻步」；這裡是說周圍美好的自然景象反而讓他感到自卑和不自在。
generatedExample: The steep climb daunted the new hikers.

explanationZhTw: 「etiolated」原本指因缺少陽光而變得蒼白、細弱；在這裡是更文學化的用法，表示他像被室內生活削弱了，顯得萎靡蒼白。
generatedExample: The plant looked etiolated after weeks in the dark.

explanationZhTw: 「broad daylight」是固定說法，意思是「大白天、光天化日之下」。
generatedExample: The robbery happened in broad daylight.

explanationZhTw: 「knoll」是「小丘、小土坡」；比一般的 hill 更小、更低。
generatedExample: They sat on a knoll and watched the sunset.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Here we are` 不是單純字面上的「我們在這裡」，而是口語裡常用來表示「我們到了」「就在這裡了」或「事情就是這樣」。
generatedExample: Here we are at the old station, exactly as planned.

explanationZhTw: 「at several paces' distance」表示「相隔好幾步遠」，是比較文學化的距離說法；在這裡強調兩人之間還有一段明顯距離。
generatedExample: She stood at several paces' distance from the door, waiting for him to open it.

explanationZhTw: 「As yet」表示「到目前為止／暫時還」，帶有「目前狀況仍未改變」的意思。
generatedExample: As yet, we have not received any reply from the manager.

explanationZhTw: 「dare」在這裡是「敢；膽敢」的意思，表示因為緊張、害怕或顧慮而不敢做某事。
generatedExample: I did not dare ask her the question.

explanationZhTw: 「mike」在這裡不是人名，而是口語中的「麥克風」；屬於較口語、帶情境色彩的用法。
generatedExample: She whispered because she thought a mike might be hidden in the room.

explanationZhTw: 「swine」字面是「豬」，但這裡用來辱罵人，意思是「討厭的傢伙、卑鄙的人」。
generatedExample: That swine lied to everyone about the missing money.

explanationZhTw: 「courage」是「勇氣」的意思。這裡指他沒有勇氣去接近她或跟她說話。
generatedExample: She finally found the courage to speak in front of the class.

explanationZhTw: 「approach」在這裡不是「方法」，而是「接近、上前搭話」的意思。
generatedExample: A stranger approached me at the train station.

explanationZhTw: 「all right」在口語裡常表示「沒問題、還好、可以」。這句裡是他笨拙地確認「我們這裡沒事吧？」的意思。
generatedExample: Are you all right after the accident?

explanationZhTw: `cut down` 是片語動詞，這裡指「把樹砍倒／砍伐」。文中用被動語態，表示這些樹曾經被砍掉。
generatedExample: The old tree was cut down last winter.

explanationZhTw: `sprout up again` 表示「重新長出來、又冒出來」。這裡是樹被砍後又再生長。
generatedExample: The weeds sprouted up again after the rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `forest` 不是字面上的森林，而是比喻成「一大片密集的木桿」。屬於帶有畫面感的非常用法。
generatedExample: From the road, the antenna towers looked like a forest of poles.

explanationZhTw: `mike` 是口語寫法，指 `microphone`（麥克風）。這裡是在說沒有地方可藏竊聽用的麥克風。
generatedExample: The reporter held the mike close to the speaker.

explanationZhTw: `make conversation` 表示「寒暄、閒聊」，重點不是交換重要資訊，而是維持氣氛或避免沉默。
generatedExample: She made conversation with the driver while they waited at the station.

explanationZhTw: `upright` 在這裡是「挺直地、端正地」；不是指道德上的「正直」，而是描述站姿。
generatedExample: He sat upright when the teacher called his name.

explanationZhTw: `ironical` 是較文學或正式的說法，表示「帶諷刺意味的、略帶反諷的」。
generatedExample: Her ironical comment made everyone in the room pause.

explanationZhTw: `cascaded` 在這裡不是字面上的「水流傾瀉」，而是比喻花朵「成串垂落、傾瀉般落下」。
generatedExample: Her hair cascaded over her shoulders.

explanationZhTw: `of their own accord` 是慣用語，表示「自然而然地、自己就發生了」，強調沒有外力介入。
generatedExample: The door opened of its own accord in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，用來引出一件很難相信或很意外的事，帶有「你敢信嗎」的語氣。
generatedExample: Would you believe, I ran into my old teacher at the airport yesterday?

explanationZhTw: `shade` 在這裡不是「陰影」，而是指顏色的深淺或色調。
generatedExample: Her jacket is a light shade of green.

explanationZhTw: `bear` 這裡是「忍受、承受」的意思，`can you bear to...` 表示「你能否忍受做某事」。
generatedExample: I can't bear to look at the mess on my desk.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `easily` 表示「輕鬆地、毫不費力地」，在對話中也帶有「當然可以、很簡單」的語氣。
generatedExample: She solved the puzzle easily in a few minutes.

explanationZhTw: `get rid of` 表示「除掉、擺脫、甩掉」。這裡是說話者在抱怨自己無法擺脫妻子，帶有黑色幽默的語氣。
generatedExample: I need to get rid of this old sofa before Friday.

explanationZhTw: `varicose veins` 是「靜脈曲張」，指腿部等部位的靜脈腫脹、扭曲，屬於醫療相關詞彙。
generatedExample: My grandmother has varicose veins and needs to rest her legs often.

explanationZhTw: `false teeth` 指「假牙」，這裡不是「假的牙齒」字面意思，而是可拆卸或人工製作的牙齒。
generatedExample: He took out his false teeth before going to bed.

explanationZhTw: 片語「couldn't care less」表示「完全不在乎、毫不在意」。這裡是說女孩對這件事沒有任何興趣或關心。
generatedExample: I couldn't care less what he thinks about my choice.

explanationZhTw: 「incredulity」是「難以置信、懷疑」；「sheer」在這裡加強語氣，表示完全不敢相信，屬於較書面的字彙。
generatedExample: She stared at the email in sheer incredulity.

explanationZhTw: 「youthful」指「年輕的、帶有青春氣息的」，比單純的 young 更有文學感。
generatedExample: Her youthful body moved quickly across the stage.

explanationZhTw: 這是親暱的稱呼，意思接近「寶貝、心肝」，用來表達非常親密的感情。
generatedExample: He whispered, "Good night, precious one."

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「完全不反抗、順從地任由對方擺布」，是較文學化的用法。
generatedExample: He noticed that the crowd was unresisting and quiet.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是泛指「感覺」，而是偏向「身體上的感官反應、觸覺感受」。
generatedExample: After the injury, she lost all physical sensation in her hand.

explanationZhTw: 指「生理上的慾望」；這裡強調的是肉體吸引，而不是情緒或精神層面的喜歡。
generatedExample: He admitted that he felt no physical desire at all.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「自己站起來／爬起來」，不是字面上的「撿起自己」。
generatedExample: After the fall, she picked herself up and kept running.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不急、慢慢來就好」，是常見安撫對方的說法。
generatedExample: There's no hurry, so take your time.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「極好的、很棒的」，語氣比一般的 good 更正式一點。
generatedExample: The view from the hill was splendid.

explanationZhTw: 指「藏身處、秘密躲藏的地方」，是帶有隱密意味的名詞。
generatedExample: They found a hide-out near the river.

explanationZhTw: 表示「迷路了」，這裡是常見的片語用法。
generatedExample: I got lost on the way to the station.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「來」，而是指「有人接近、過來」，屬於依上下文理解的用法。
generatedExample: If the bus was coming, we could hear it before it reached the stop.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `yours` 不是單純指「你的東西」，而是省略了前文所指的內容，意思接近「你的名字／你的那個資訊」。在對話中這種用法很常見，屬於代詞的語境用法。
generatedExample: I know yours, but I forgot mine.

explanationZhTw: `find out` 是片語動詞，意思是「查出、得知、發現（某事的真相）」。這裡問的是對方怎麼知道這件事。
generatedExample: I need to find out who sent the message.

explanationZhTw: 「找出、弄清楚」；這裡指「我比較會查明事情真相／把事情問清楚」。
generatedExample: She is good at finding things out quickly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「昂貴的」，而是說話時對對方的稱呼，帶有親暱、輕鬆，甚至可能帶點諷刺意味。
generatedExample: Come in and sit down, dear.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「對……有什麼想法／怎麼看待……」；也就是在問對方以前怎麼看自己。
generatedExample: What did you think of the movie?

explanationZhTw: 「temptation」在這裡是「誘惑、衝動」的意思；整句表示他沒有想要對她說謊的衝動。
generatedExample: She resisted the temptation to check her phone during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「a sort of」在這裡不是字面上的「一種」，而是用來表示說話者帶有保留、近似或不完全確定的語氣，相當於「某種程度上算是」。
generatedExample: It was a sort of apology, but not a very direct one.

explanationZhTw: 「love-offering」在這裡是比較少見的用法，指「表達愛意或善意的舉動／奉獻」，不是日常最常見的字面組合。
generatedExample: His donation felt like a love-offering to the community that had helped him.

explanationZhTw: 「start off」是片語動詞，意思是「一開始、先從……開始」。這裡指先從說最糟的事開始。
generatedExample: We started off the trip with a quick coffee stop.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `the sight of you` 不是單純指「景象」，而是指「看到你這件事／你的樣子」，帶有厭惡感。
generatedExample: The sight of him walking into the room made everyone uncomfortable.

explanationZhTw: `thought seriously of` 表示「認真考慮過要做某事」，不是隨口一想。
generatedExample: She seriously thought of quitting her job after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `cobblestone` 指鋪路用的圓石或鵝卵石，常見於老街或石板路。
generatedExample: The old market street was paved with cobblestones.

explanationZhTw: `the Thought Police` 是小說中的專有名詞，指會監控、懲罰不被允許思想的秘密警察。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Thought Police monitor what people think and say.

explanationZhTw: adv. 高興地、欣喜地。這裡表示她笑得很開心，帶著得意的感受。
generatedExample: She nodded delightedly when she heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 tribute 不是指正式的「致敬儀式」，而是指「讚美、稱許」。
generatedExample: He took the teacher's comment as a tribute to his hard work.

explanationZhTw: n. 偽裝、喬裝。這裡指她用來隱藏真實身分或外表的裝扮。
generatedExample: His disguise fooled everyone at the party.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Thought Police」不是一般字面上的「思想警察」，而是《一九八四》中的專有名詞，指監視並打壓人們思想與言論的國家機器。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Thought Police can arrest people for disloyal thoughts.

explanationZhTw: 「general appearance」指一個人整體外表給人的印象，重點是看起來怎麼樣，而不是某個單一部位。
generatedExample: Her general appearance made her look older than she really was.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「fresh」不是「新鮮」或「剛做好的」，而是指「精神好、看起來有活力、年輕有朝氣」。
generatedExample: After a short break, she felt fresh and ready to continue.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「忠於執政黨的人／黨內成員」，帶有政治忠誠的意味，不只是一般的政黨會員。
generatedExample: As a Party member, he was expected to follow every rule.

explanationZhTw: 固定說法，表示言行一致、表面和內心都很「純正／忠誠」。
generatedExample: She tried to be pure in word and deed, not just in public speeches.

explanationZhTw: 指有組織地前進的遊行隊伍，常見於公共活動、慶典或政治場合。
generatedExample: The town held processions to celebrate the festival.

explanationZhTw: 標語；口號，通常是簡短、容易記的宣傳語。
generatedExample: The protesters carried signs with short slogans.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「社區健行」，而是政權安排的集體戶外活動，帶有社會控制或宣傳色彩。
generatedExample: The school organized community hikes to keep everyone involved.

explanationZhTw: 口語省略說法，意思是「以及其他類似的東西」。
generatedExample: We talked about sports, music, and all that stuff.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「只要有一點點機會」；這裡強調機會非常小。
generatedExample: If I had a quarter of a chance, I would leave this job immediately.

explanationZhTw: 公開檢舉、指控，通常帶有強烈批評或政治意味。
generatedExample: He denounced the plan as unfair.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的政治造詞，指「思想違反政權規範的人」；不是一般法律用語。
generatedExample: In the novel, anyone who disagreed could be labeled a thought-criminal.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡表示「把某人殺掉／除掉」，語氣帶有徹底消滅的意思。
generatedExample: The villain tried to kill off anyone who knew the truth.

explanationZhTw: 指「那一類、那種東西／情況」，語氣比較含糊，用來避免說得太具體。
generatedExample: I was looking for something of that kind, not an exact copy.

explanationZhTw: 表示「很多、相當多」，比單純的 many 更有強調語氣。
generatedExample: A great many people agreed with the proposal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是對話中的填充語，常用來讓語氣更口語，或暗示說話者認為對方能理解。
generatedExample: It was a bit awkward, you know, but we managed it.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語中的髒話式強調語，表示很惱火、厭煩或強烈不滿。
generatedExample: This bloody thing never works when I need it.

explanationZhTw: 快速用力扯下來；這裡表示動作很突然、很粗暴。
generatedExample: He was ripping off his coat as soon as he got inside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「製造」，而是「拿出、掏出」某樣東西，屬於情境上較不直觀的用法。
generatedExample: She produced a notebook from her bag and started writing.

explanationZhTw: 指一大塊厚實的巧克力，像磚塊一樣的「整塊」而不是一般薄片。
generatedExample: He broke off a slab of chocolate and shared it with everyone.

explanationZhTw: 容易碎成小屑的；這裡用來形容食物質地。
generatedExample: The cake was dry and crumbly after being left out all night.

explanationZhTw: 一陣淡淡的氣味、輕微的聞到的味道。
generatedExample: I caught a whiff of smoke from the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 引發、喚起；這裡表示氣味喚起了記憶。
generatedExample: Her old song stirred up memories of school days.

explanationZhTw: 確切說明、弄清楚；這裡表示他無法準確想起那段記憶。
generatedExample: I can’t quite pin down why the plan feels wrong.

explanationZhTw: `stuff` 是口語用法，意思是「東西、物品、這些玩意兒」，比 `things` 更隨意，也帶點不精確的感覺。
generatedExample: I need to pack my stuff before we leave.

explanationZhTw: 指非法買賣或走私交易的市場，不是字面上的「黑色市場」。
generatedExample: They bought the medicine on the black market.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指青少年團體中的帶領者，不是軍隊裡的部隊長。
generatedExample: She was a troop-leader when she was sixteen.

explanationZhTw: 指不拿薪水、出於自願去做的工作。
generatedExample: He spends his weekends doing voluntary work at the clinic.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語用法，意思是「胡說、廢話」，不是植物腐爛。
generatedExample: I am tired of hearing that rot from him.

explanationZhTw: 指遊行隊伍、列隊前進的群眾行列。
generatedExample: The streets were crowded with processions after the ceremony.

explanationZhTw: 指逃避、偷懶，不願承擔該做的事。
generatedExample: She never shirks difficult tasks.

explanationZhTw: fragment 是「片段、碎片」；這裡指一小塊巧克力，不是抽象的片段。
generatedExample: She broke the cookie into a small fragment and shared it with me.

explanationZhTw: delightful 是「令人愉快的、很美味的」；這裡形容巧克力的味道非常好。
generatedExample: We had a delightful dinner by the sea.

explanationZhTw: consciousness 是「意識」；此處重點是心裡能感覺到、但還不完全清楚的心理狀態。
generatedExample: After the accident, he slowly returned to full consciousness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「可縮減」；意思是這段記憶無法被整理成清楚、固定的形狀或畫面，仍然很模糊。
generatedExample: Her idea was real, but it was not yet reducible to definite shape.

explanationZhTw: idiom，意思是「用眼角餘光看到」；表示不是正面、清楚地看見，而是模糊地注意到。
generatedExample: I saw someone waving out of the corner of my eye.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，不是真的用手推開；意思是他把那個念頭或記憶從腦中趕走。
generatedExample: Whenever the thought came back, she pushed it away from her.

explanationZhTw: undo 在這裡是「挽回、取消、把已做過的事撤回」；不是單純把東西解開。
generatedExample: He wished he could undo his mistake.

explanationZhTw: 年輕；這裡是基本形容詞，表示年紀不大。
generatedExample: She is still very young, but she learns quickly.

explanationZhTw: 比較級「更年輕」；用來比較年齡差距。
generatedExample: My brother is three years younger than I am.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「吸引你、讓你有好感」的意思，不只是一般的「吸引」。
generatedExample: The quiet confidence in his voice seemed to attract her.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「冒險試試看、碰碰運氣」。
generatedExample: We should take a chance and apply for the job.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「察覺、辨認出」的意思，不是單純指「看見」某個東西。
generatedExample: She is good at spotting small mistakes in reports.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「不屬於這個地方／不合群、格格不入」的意思。
generatedExample: At that party, I felt like I didn't belong.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 THEM 帶有特定政治或陣營指涉，意思是「站在他們的對立面、反對那一方」。
generatedExample: He was quietly against THEM from the start.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「swear」是指「說髒話、罵粗話」，不是「發誓」。
generatedExample: He never swore in front of children.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「swear」是指「說髒話、罵粗話」，不是「發誓」。
generatedExample: He never swore in front of children.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示「無論如何；至少；總之」。
generatedExample: It was late, but we should leave at any rate.

explanationZhTw: 「Inner Party」指黨內最核心、最高階層的菁英成員，在這裡是專有的政治術語。
generatedExample: The Inner Party made the final decision on the policy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「chalked up」是字面意思，指「用粉筆寫上去」。
generatedExample: Someone chalked up a warning on the wall.

explanationZhTw: 「two abreast」表示「並排、肩並肩地」。
generatedExample: The hikers walked two abreast along the trail.

explanationZhTw: 「sash」是繫在腰間或身上的帶子、腰帶。
generatedExample: She tied a blue sash around her dress.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「安靜地走；不發出聲音地行動」，強調小心和低調。
generatedExample: We should go quietly so we do not wake the baby.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `the open` 不是單純形容詞「開著的」，而是指「空曠、沒有遮蔽物的地方」；整句是在說不要走到容易被看見的地方。
generatedExample: We stayed in the open too long and the rain started before we reached the shelter.

explanationZhTw: `watching` 這裡是「在監視、盯著看」的意思，不只是一般的「看」，帶有警戒或偷偷觀察的感覺。
generatedExample: Someone may be watching the house from across the street.

explanationZhTw: `boughs` 指樹木較大的「樹枝、粗枝」，屬於比一般常用字更文學或正式的說法。
generatedExample: The bird hid among the boughs above our heads.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「經歷、承受」，比一般口語 `go through` 更正式、帶書面感。
generatedExample: She underwent a sudden change in mood after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `close-bitten` 描述草地被牛羊等動物啃得很短，屬於比較文學性的說法。
generatedExample: The sheep had left the hillside close-bitten and bare.

explanationZhTw: `perceptibly` 表示「明顯到能察覺、勉強看得出來地」，是偏正式的副詞。
generatedExample: The room cooled perceptibly after the window was opened.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「看不見、在視線之外」。
generatedExample: The cottage is just out of sight behind the trees.

explanationZhTw: `dace` 是一種小型淡水魚名，屬於專門名詞。
generatedExample: We watched dace dart through the clear water.

explanationZhTw: n. 溪流；小河。這裡指附近是否有一條水流。
generatedExample: We sat beside a stream and had lunch.

explanationZhTw: v. 低聲說；耳語。這裡表示說話音量很小，像是在偷偷詢問。
generatedExample: He whispered the plan to his friend.

explanationZhTw: 指「溪流、小河」，是常見的自然地貌詞。
generatedExample: We crossed the stream on a small wooden bridge.

explanationZhTw: 指「邊緣、邊界」，這裡是田地的旁邊。
generatedExample: The old house is at the edge of the forest.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是在強調「實際上」的對比，而是口語中用來補充或修正資訊，意思接近「其實、說真的」。
generatedExample: Actually, I think the blue one looks better.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 lying 不是「說謊」，而是「靜靜待著、躺著」；整個片語是在描述魚安靜地待在水池裡。
generatedExample: The cats were lying in the sun by the window.

explanationZhTw: 指「柳樹」，是常見的植物名稱。
generatedExample: We sat under the willow trees and had lunch.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「金色國度」，而是指他心中理想化、近乎夢境般的鄉野／樂土意象。
generatedExample: For a moment, the valley felt like the Golden Country he had always imagined.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「金色國家」，而是指一個帶有理想化、想像色彩的地方名稱或意象。
generatedExample: She kept thinking about the Golden Country as a place where life could finally be peaceful.

explanationZhTw: 這是口語中的淡化說法，表示「沒什麼、真的不重要」，語氣比字面上的 nothing 更委婉。
generatedExample: The scratch is just cosmetic; it's nothing, really.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是感嘆語，用來引起注意，意思接近「喂／你看」，不是單純的動詞「看」。
generatedExample: Look, I need to show you something important.

explanationZhTw: 指「低聲說、耳語」，表示用很小的聲音說話。
generatedExample: She whispered the news so no one else could hear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指鳥「落在」樹枝上；比一般的 `land` 更偏書面語。
generatedExample: A pigeon alighted on the statue and stayed there for a minute.

explanationZhTw: 樹的大枝幹或粗枝。這個字比 `branch` 更文學、較少口語。
generatedExample: The owl sat quietly on a bough above the river.

explanationZhTw: 正式的鞠躬、致敬；這裡是擬人化寫法，表示鳥好像在向太陽行禮。
generatedExample: He gave a quick obeisance before stepping onto the stage.

explanationZhTw: 源源不絕地湧出、傾瀉而出。常用來形容聲音、話語或情感大量流出。
generatedExample: She poured forth questions the moment the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 寂靜、靜默；這裡是名詞，指整個下午的安靜氣氛。
generatedExample: A hush fell over the room when the announcement was made.

explanationZhTw: 炫耀、賣弄；帶有故意展示自己本事的意思。
generatedExample: He kept showing off in front of the new students.

explanationZhTw: 高超技巧；通常用來稱讚樂器演奏、表演或某種技藝非常精湛。
generatedExample: Her virtuosity on the piano surprised even the judges.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「樂器」，而是指監聽裝置、麥克風或類似設備。屬於依上下文才看得出的意思。
generatedExample: They discovered the instrument was hidden behind the bookshelf.

explanationZhTw: 逐漸地、慢慢地。這是較固定的說法，語氣比 `gradually` 更文學。
generatedExample: By degrees, the crowd quieted down and listened.

explanationZhTw: 透過、穿過後滲入。這裡形容陽光穿過樹葉。
generatedExample: Soft light filtered through the curtains at dawn.

explanationZhTw: 胸貼胸，表示身體非常接近、緊緊擁抱。
generatedExample: The dancers moved breast to breast in the final pose.

explanationZhTw: 柔軟可變形、容易屈服；這裡形容觸感很軟，很順從。
generatedExample: The fabric was smooth and yielding under her fingers.

explanationZhTw: 受到驚嚇；忽然害怕起來。這是固定搭配，語氣比單用 `was frightened` 更生動。
generatedExample: The dog took fright at the thunder and ran inside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「NOW」不是普通的時間副詞，而是帶有催促、命令意味的「立刻、現在就做」。大寫加上語境讓語氣更強。
generatedExample: Now, open the door before they notice us.

explanationZhTw: 「whispered」表示「低聲說、耳語」。這裡強調說話音量非常小，通常是為了不讓別人聽見。
generatedExample: She whispered the answer so no one else could hear.

explanationZhTw: 「hide-out」指秘密藏身處、躲避他人的地方；在這裡是角色建議回到更安全的藏匿點。
generatedExample: We stayed in a hide-out until the police had gone.

explanationZhTw: 表示「穿過、曲折前進」，不是字面上的「穿線」。這裡描寫兩人沿著樹叢間往回走。
generatedExample: We threaded our way through the crowd to reach the exit.

explanationZhTw: saplings 指「幼樹、樹苗」；ring of saplings 是一圈幼樹，形成包圍的場景。
generatedExample: The cabin was hidden behind a ring of saplings.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「在嘴角周圍重新出現」，而是說她的微笑又浮現出來。
generatedExample: A tired smile reappeared round the corners of his mouth.

explanationZhTw: flung aside 表示「猛地把……甩到一旁」；帶出快速而有力的動作。
generatedExample: He flung his bag aside and ran to the door.

explanationZhTw: anchored 原意是「用錨固定」；這裡比喻成「視線被牢牢吸住、移不開」。
generatedExample: Her eyes were anchored by the bright red sign across the street.

explanationZhTw: 表示「當然／沒錯」，是常見回應語，用來表示同意或強調事情很明顯，不是字面上的「照課程那樣」意思。
generatedExample: Of course I can help you with that.

explanationZhTw: 「scores of times」是慣用說法，意思是「很多次、數十次」，比字面上的 scores 更像是在強調次數很多。
generatedExample: I've told him scores of times to be careful.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 anyway 不是「無論如何」的動作副詞，而是對話中的收尾／轉折語氣詞，帶有「反正、總之」的意思。
generatedExample: Anyway, we should leave before it gets dark.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Party` 不是「派對」，而是《一九八四》裡的政治組織「黨」，指執政黨成員。
generatedExample: He was afraid of being seen with Party members.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Party members` 指的是「黨員、政黨成員」，不是一般的派對參加者。
generatedExample: He was only allowed to meet with Party members after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 《內部黨》（Inner Party）是《一九八四》中的專有名詞，指統治奧威爾世界的最高權力菁英階層，不是一般日常英文中的字面「內部政黨」意思。
generatedExample: The protagonist was terrified of being noticed by the Inner Party.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「豬」，而是罵人的說法，意思是「卑鄙的人、討厭的人」。
generatedExample: He called his rivals swine after they lied to him.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「只要有一點機會／一有機會就會……」。
generatedExample: If the gates were left open, the dog would run out if it got half a chance.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「裝作、聲稱自己是」，帶有「表面上看起來如此」的意思。
generatedExample: He is not as confident as he makes out.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示他突然感到強烈的喜悅、興奮或激動，不是真的心臟跳起來。
generatedExample: My heart leapt when I saw the acceptance email.

explanationZhTw: 表示「很多次、數十次以上」，用來強調次數非常多。
generatedExample: I have told him scores of times to lock the door.

explanationZhTw: `hint at` 是片語動詞，意思是「暗示、含蓄地表示」。
generatedExample: Her pause hinted at a bigger problem.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「表面之下」，這裡指外表看不出來、但實際上隱藏著的狀況。
generatedExample: She seemed calm, but under the surface she was furious.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「假象、偽裝、騙局」，不是一般物件，而是用來掩飾真相的東西。
generatedExample: The apology was just a sham.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「邪惡、不義、罪惡行為」，屬於較正式且偏文學的詞。
generatedExample: The novel exposes the iniquity of the system.

explanationZhTw: `pull down` 在這裡是片語動詞，表示「把某人拉下來、拉倒較低的位置」。
generatedExample: He pulled the box down from the shelf.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `you've had` 不是單純「你擁有過」，而是委婉地指你曾經交往過、發生過關係的男人；在這句話裡帶有強烈的控制、占有與羞辱語氣。
generatedExample: He spoke coldly about the women she'd had, even though it was none of his business.

explanationZhTw: `understand` 是常見動詞，意思是「理解、明白」。
generatedExample: Do you understand the instructions?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `perfectly` 不是指「完美地」，而是表示「完全可以、當然、沒問題」的語氣，用來肯定對方的說法。
generatedExample: “Can you finish it today?” “Yes, perfectly.”

explanationZhTw: 指「純潔、純淨、道德上的潔淨」。這裡不是單指乾淨，而是帶有價值判斷的抽象概念。
generatedExample: They see innocence and purity as something fragile.

explanationZhTw: 指「善良、良善」或「道德上的好」。這裡是抽象名詞，不是單純的「好吃」或「很好」。
generatedExample: Her goodness made everyone feel welcome.

explanationZhTw: 指「美德、德行」。在這句話裡是指人應該具備的正面道德品質。
generatedExample: Honesty is a virtue that matters in every job.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，意思是「壞到骨子裡、徹底腐敗」。強調某人或某事的邪惡或墮落非常徹底。
generatedExample: He was corrupt to the bones and could not be trusted.

explanationZhTw: 表示「應該、理當」；這裡帶有一種略帶諷刺或自嘲的語氣，意思是「那我應該會很合你胃口／很適合你」。
generatedExample: You ought to finish your homework before dinner.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是單純指「西裝」，而是指「適合你、合你的口味、讓你滿意」。
generatedExample: This color should suit you well.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是稱呼語，表示對對方的親暱、諷刺或客氣，類似中文的「親愛的／老兄／小姐」。
generatedExample: Thank you, dear, for your help.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，意思是「壞到骨子裡、從根本上就很腐敗／墮落」。這裡不是字面上的身體腐爛，而是強烈的道德自我評價。
generatedExample: He joked that he was lazy to the bones after a long holiday.

explanationZhTw: 「simply」在這裡表示「只是、單單」，用來排除其他解讀。這種用法很常見，但在句中是拿來縮小範圍，強調「不只是我」。
generatedExample: I am not simply tired; I am completely exhausted.

explanationZhTw: 「in itself」在這裡不是單純字面上的「本身裡面」，而是「就其本質而言、單就這件事本身」。作者在強調他問的是行為/事情本身的性質，不是某個人。
generatedExample: The movie was confusing, but in itself it was fascinating.

explanationZhTw: 「adore」表示非常喜愛、熱愛某人或某物，語氣比一般的「like」更強烈，帶有明顯的情感投入。
generatedExample: She adores her new piano.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「徹底摧毀、把……撕成碎片式地瓦解」；這裡不是字面上的撕開，而是指這種原始欲望足以讓政黨崩潰。
generatedExample: The scandal could tear the whole company to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，這裡指「分開、彼此鬆開」；在此情境中是身體上的分離，不是情緒或關係破裂。
generatedExample: After the dance, they laughed and fell apart.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `discarded` 是「被丟下、棄置不用的」；用來形容脫下後放在一旁的工作褲。
generatedExample: He picked up the discarded ticket from the floor.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞 `pull over` 在這裡是「把衣物拉過來蓋在某人身上」；不是開車停到路邊的 `pull over`。
generatedExample: She pulled the blanket partly over the child.

explanationZhTw: 「freckled face」指長滿雀斑的臉；`freckled` 是描述外觀的常用形容詞。
generatedExample: Her freckled face lit up when she laughed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `pillowed` 是動詞的特殊用法，表示「像枕著一樣托放著」；意思是她的臉靠在手掌上。
generatedExample: He pillowed his head on the folded jacket and rested.

explanationZhTw: `occurred to him` 表示「他突然想到／意識到」，不是「發生在他身上」。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I had left the keys at home.

explanationZhTw: `surname` 指姓氏，也就是家族的姓，不是名字。
generatedExample: I know her first name, but I do not know her surname.

explanationZhTw: `helpless` 表示「無助的、無法自理的」。這裡是在睡眠中的身體呈現脆弱感，帶出說話者的憐惜與保護慾。
generatedExample: She felt helpless after losing her phone and wallet on the train.

explanationZhTw: `pitying` 是「同情的、帶憐憫的」。這裡不是中性的描述，而是帶有情感色彩，表示他對她產生了憐惜。
generatedExample: He gave me a pitying smile when I failed the easy quiz.

explanationZhTw: `mindless` 在這裡不是「沒有頭腦」的粗俗說法，而是指不經思考、近乎本能的；`mindless tenderness` 指一種不帶理性分析的溫柔。
generatedExample: The song brought back a mindless tenderness for my childhood home.

explanationZhTw: `flank` 原意是「側面、肋側」。這裡是人體或動物身體的一側，不是軍事用法。
generatedExample: The nurse checked the patient's flank for any sign of injury.

explanationZhTw: `desirable` 表示「有吸引力的、值得渴望的」。這裡是性吸引力的意思，不只是一般的「令人喜歡」。
generatedExample: The apartment is small, but its location is highly desirable.

explanationZhTw: `mixed up with` 是片語動詞，意思是「和……混在一起、夾雜著」。這裡表示情感不純粹，而是被恐懼和仇恨摻雜進去。
generatedExample: Her excitement was mixed up with anxiety before the interview.

explanationZhTw: `embrace` 這裡是名詞，表示「擁抱」，而不是常見的動詞「接受、擁護」。語氣帶有文學性。
generatedExample: Their embrace lasted only a second, but it felt meaningful.

explanationZhTw: `climax` 指「高潮、最強烈或最關鍵的部分」。這裡是把親密行為寫成一場衝突的最高點。
generatedExample: The climax of the movie was more emotional than I expected.

explanationZhTw: `the Party` 在這裡不是一般的「政黨」泛稱，而是特定的政治統治集團，屬於《一九八四》的專有政治語境。
generatedExample: Speaking freely was dangerous because the Party controlled everything.
